display_name: Landlord-Tenant Legal Notice (Eviction / Rent Arrears / Deposit Refund / Essential Services)
structural_summary: A formal legal notice exchanged between landlord and tenant (or licensor and licensee), sent directly or through an advocate. Layout: sender/advocate block; date; mode of dispatch (Registered Post A.D.); addressee block; subject line identifying the premises and grievance; salutation; numbered paragraphs reciting the tenancy (premises, commencement date, monthly rent, security deposit), the default or grievance with dates, and prior reminders; the operative demand (vacate and hand over possession, pay arrears, refund deposit, restore essential supplies, issue rent receipts, or desist from harassment) within a stated compliance period of 15 or 30 days; consequences of non-compliance (eviction or possession suit, recovery proceedings, costs); reservation of rights; signature block of the party or advocate.
statutory_requirements:
- Identify the parties and relationship (landlord/licensor, tenant/licensee), the premises by full address, monthly rent or licence fee, tenancy commencement date, and security deposit if any.
- Recite the default or grievance with dates: months of arrears, expiry of the term, date essential supplies were disconnected, or date the tenant vacated and handed over keys.
- A landlord terminating a month-to-month tenancy must give 15 days' notice under Section 106, Transfer of Property Act, 1882.
- Where a state rent law covers the premises, statutory grounds and procedure may override Section 106 — [VERIFY: applicable state rent control act, e.g. Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999] before pleading statutory grounds.
- For restoration of essential supplies (water/electricity), rely on the agreement and [VERIFY: provision of the applicable state rent act penalising withdrawal of essential supplies].
- Security-deposit refund demands are contractual: cite the agreement clause, the vacation/handover date, and any agreed interest; no statute need be cited.
- State the operative demand precisely (vacate and hand over vacant possession / pay arrears / refund deposit / restore supply / issue rent receipts / desist from harassment) within a stated period of 15 or 30 days.
- State consequences of non-compliance: eviction or possession suit, recovery proceedings with interest, or other civil/criminal remedies, at the addressee's risk as to costs.
- Record mode of dispatch (Registered Post A.D. recommended) and preserve proof of service.

fields.name: security_deposit
fields.description: Security deposit, 2-10 months of the monthly rent (kept consistent with monthly_rent, not drawn independently)

fields.name: vacation_date
fields.description: Date the tenant vacated and handed over keys; only for deposit-refund/deduction scenarios. Chained off tenancy_start_date so it follows commencement; past-anchored when the start date is withheld.

fields.name: arrears_months
fields.placeholder: [NUMBER OF MONTHS OF RENT IN ARREARS]

fields.name: notice_period_days
fields.description: Compliance window demanded in the notice; 15 aligns with Section 106 TPA for month-to-month termination.
